content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660       322-5660          322-5901             322-6441           322-6444

March 18, 1982


Robert S. Allen, Director
Bay Area Rapid Transit District
800 Madison Street
Oakland, CA 94607

Re:  Advice Request
     No. 82-1-004

Dear Mr. Allen:

     Thank you for your letter requesting advice from this
office. The following advice is provided pursuant to
Government Code Section 83114(b).1/

     From your letter and our phone conversation, my
understanding of the situation is as follows. You are the
elected Director for Election District 5 of the Bay Area
Rapid Transit District ("BART").

     You are also employed by Southern Pacific
Transportation Company ("SPT") as a clerk in Oakland and own
50 shares of SPT stock worth approximately $1,500. SPT
operates a commuter service between San Francisco and
San Jose.

     Next year you will be President of the BART Board of
Directors. You asked whether you would have a conflict of
interest by calling hearings, initiating studies and
correspondence and otherwise beginning the process toward a
unified Bay Area transit system. What is contemplated is an
"Around the Bay" loop. To complete the loop, you envision
adding San Mateo and Santa Clara counties to the BART
operation and purchasing the SPT commuter facilities and
operation.

     You also explained to me that BART will not be able to
consider spending for extensions outside the present
district until all extensions are completed within the
district. It is estimated that these extensions will not be

________________
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code.

* changes made pursuant to phone conversation w/ Allen
3/25/82 dmp.

Robert S. Allen, Director
March 18, 1982
Page Two


completed for at least 10 to 15 years. You estimate that
the overall plan, which you intend to advocate, could not be
realized for 20 to 40 years.

Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act contains the
basic prohibition against conflicts of interest:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest.

An official has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one
thousand dollars ($1,000).

. . .

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard
to official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior
to the time when the decision is made.

Section 87103.

Under the facts presented, the governmental decisions
which you are now asking about -- decisions to undertake
studies or hold hearings -- will not foreseeably have a
financial effect on SPT. The governmental decisions which
could materially affect SPT, such as the decision to
purchase the commuter line, will be made, if ever, in the
distant future. In addition, a decision to purchase the
commuter line is contingent on many other governmental
decisions and events. Accordingly, there is not a
substantial likelihood or probability that SPT will be
affected by the decisions to call for hearings, request
studies and otherwise work toward a unified transit system

Robert S. Allen, Director
March 18, 1982
Page Three


in the Bay Area. See Opinion requested by Tom Thorner,
1 FPPC Opinions 198 (No. 75-089, December 4, 1975).
Therefore, you are not required to disqualify yourself from
any of these activities.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me.

Very truly yours,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh

BART                    BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
                          800 Madison Street
                          Oakland, California 94607
                          Telephone (415) 465-4100  ext. 521


                                                    JAK 3 8 UE AM U.
                                                    January 4, 1982


Legal Division, FPPC
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

EUGENE GARFINKLE
    PRESIDENT

ARTHUR J. SHARTSIS
    VICE-PRESIDENT

KEITH BERNARD
    GENERAL MANAGER


                          Friends:

                              I seek your advice on a potential conflict of interest
                          problem I face as a public official.

                              As an elected BART director, I represent over 4 million
                          people in BART Election District 5.  BART serves three counties:
                          San Francisco, Alameda, and Contra Costa.

DIRECTORS

BARCLAY SIMPSON
    1ST DISTRICT
                              I am employed by Southern Pacific Transportation Company
NELLO BIANCO
    2ND DISTRICT      (SPT) and own under 100 shares of SP stock bought through
ARTHUR J. SHARTSIS    payroll deduction.  Furloughed October 1 in an SPT Engineering
    3RD DISTRICT      Department RIF, I now work as an SPT clerk in Oakland.  I have
                      no effective say in any of SP's corporate decisions.
MARGARET K. PRYOR

ROBERT S. ALLEN           SPT operates commute service between San Francisco and
    5TH DISTRICT      San Jose under contract with Caltrans and local agencies.  This
JOHN GLENN            commute service is a minuscule part of the total SP operation.
    6TH DISTRICT

WILFRED I. USSERY         Adding San Mateo and Santa Clara counties to the BART
    7TH DISTRICT      operation could greatly benefit public transportation in the
EUGENE GARFINKLE      Bay Area, enabling a regionally rational "San Jose - Around the
    8TH DISTRICT      Bay" loop.  Presumably such a loop would use all or part of the
JOHN H. KIRKWOOD      SPT commute facilities and operation, which SPT offered a few
    9TH DISTRICT      years back to sell to any qualified transit agency.


                              Do you see any conflict of interest problem if I:

                              1) Aggressively seek to add the two counties to BART operations?
                                  This might be by annexation or other service arrangement.
                                  By implication SPT's commute line would be involved.

                              2) Seek to influence specific BART routings which might include
                                  all or part of the present SPT commute line.


                              In good conscience I could do both, but I do not wish to
                          violate the law even in so good a cause.  Hence my request that
                          you advise me, based on the facts as stated above.


                                                    Very truly yours,

                                                    Robert S. Allen

SANTA

LOST & FOUND
A Lost and Found is located at the 12th Street Civic Center
Station. It is open Monday through Friday from 12 noon to
6 p.m. Call 465-4100 for information on lost articles.

PARKING
For BART patron parking, see the enclosed parking map. Parking is free at all stations except Lake Merritt Station where it is 25¢. Special parking spaces are reserved for
mid-day passengers and the handicapped. Parking lot restrictions are strictly enforced so please read all signs and
obey curb markings.

IN CASE OF EMERGENCY
• "Attendant Call" intercom is located at the end of each
car. Talk to train operator.
• Emergency door releases are located above seats next to
door. Pull cover panel away and move lever in the direction
of the arrow.
• If evacuation is necessary, please assist handicapped
persons. Persons in wheelchairs should be carried. Do not
evacuate without assistance. If it is necessary to leave the train, do not touch the
electrified third rail.
• Read "Emergency Procedure" poster in each car and follow instructions outlined.
• Each car has two fire extinguishers. Signs on doors
at each end of the cars mark the location. Break plastic to
remove extinguishers. Lead cars have an extinguisher in
the cab.
• Please listen for announcements and if required, evacuation instructions.

Emergency Phones
Located in the Transbay Tube, Berkeley Hills Tunnel and
subway areas are marked by a blue light. Lift receiver for
direct line to BART Central.

RIDING TIPS
1. Each rider must have a separate ticket.
2. You can not combine tickets.
3. Use the same ticket to exit as used to enter.
4. Discount tickets are available when adding fare to red or
green tickets.
5. One dollar will be automatically charged if you
neglect to insert your ticket in the exit slot when you
ride BART. If you decide to leave the station after
entering without taking a ride, see the station agent
or call us on the white courtesy telephone before
exiting through the fare gates.
6. Ticket machines will accept nickels, dimes,
quarters, 51 & 55 bills only.

The times which are published in BART brochures do not
anticipate service disruptions, but are approximations for a
normal trip. BART cannot assume responsibility for inconvenience, expense or damage resulting from errors in time
estimates, delayed trains, failure to make connection or for
changes in or shortage of equipment. Time schedule, equipment and fares shown in this document are subject to change
without notice.

All about
BART
1981

Includes fares,
travel times,
and information

BART
[logo]

800 Madison Street, Oakland, CA 94607

JUY
UY
PHONE INFORMATION
If you need information concerning BART
and connecting bus service, call us:

Fremont/Union City area ..........793-BART
Hayward/San Leandro area ..........783-BART
Livermore/Pleasanton area ...........462-BART
Oakland/Berkeley/Orinda area .....465-BART
Richmond/El Cerrito area ...........236-BART
San Francisco/Daly City area ......788-BART
South San Francisco/San Bruno area..873-BART
Lafayette/Walnut Creek/
Concord area.....................933-BART
Antioch/Pittsburg area ...........754-BART
TTY .................................639-2220

BART & BUSES AND AMTRAK
From Richmond Station, board Amtrak trains to points
east. Call 800-648-3850 for information.

In San Francisco stations, while MUNI machines sell a
two-part transfer (good on buses, trolleys, cable cars) for
50¢.

At the Daly City Station only pick up a free two-part trans
fer. Get the transfers before you exit the fare gates. Use one
part for a MUNI ride away from BART and use the other part
for the return trip to BART.

In East Bay stations served by AC Transit get a free
transfer from the white AC Transit machine before you exit
the fare gate. It's good for a free ride from BART. Pay the
regular bus fare to return to BART.

This transfer is good for a one zone ride on BART
Express buses.

BART Express Buses connect five BART stations
(Walnut Creek, Hayward, Bay Fair, Concord and El Cerrito
Del Norte) with downtown San Francisco and Oakland.

BART Express Bus fares for adults are 50¢ or 75¢ depend-
ing on how far you ride. Senior Citizens 65 and older,
Children 5-12 years old, and the handicapped with Bay
Region Transit Discount Card ride for 10¢ and 20¢. Children
4 and under ride free. Transfers from one Express Bus to
another are available from the bus driver. BART Express
buses will honor transfers from local transit lines in Western
Central and Eastern Contra Costa County.

SPECIAL SHUTTLE SERVICES
El BARTito—

Free van service, 7:30 a.m. to 6:30 p.m., Monday through
Friday from Hayward Station to County Administration
Center. 553-1144.

Humphrey Go-BART—

Free bus service, from Berkeley Station, Monday through
Friday to U.C. Berkeley Campus. 642-5149.

Walnut Creek—

Free transfer from BART's Walnut Creek Station to down-
town Walnut Creek, Monday through Saturday. 935-3300.

The Loma Ranger—

Van service, 6:30 a.m. to 8:40 a.m., 4:15 p.m. to 6:45 p.m.,
Monday through Friday, from Glen Park Station to
Miraloma Park. Fare is a 50¢ BART/Muni transfer pur-
chased at station and paid only on evening trip.

SAFETY & COMFORT

For your safety and comfort, please follow the few simple
rules that will help keep BART safe, comfortable and clean
for everyone:

• No eating, drinking or playing radios and tape recorders are not permitted in stations or on trains. Viola-
tion of the no smoking ordinance can lead to a fine of up
to $50.

• Pets completely enclosed within acceptable carrying
cases and guide or signal dogs are the only pets
permitted on BART.

• Please keep clear of the platform edge and the exterior
of the train until it comes to a complete stop in the station.

• Please do not stand in front of or beside the train.

• Please do not leave papers on trains.

• Make sure you have not left any valuables in stations or on
trains.

• Flammable liquids are not allowed in stations or on trains.

• Roller skating and riding skateboards are not allowed in
stations or on trains.

Fares

ANKS AND THEIR BRANCHES SELL MULTI-RIDE & DISCOUNT TICKETS

What is contemplated is an "Around the Bay" loop. To complete the loop, you envision adding San Mateo and Santa Clara counties to the BART operation and possibly purchasing the SPT commuter facilities and operation.
